name: Changing Units — Metres, Grams and Litres
whatIsIt: The same amount can be written in different units. 1 metre = 100 centimetres, 1 kilometre = 1000 metres, 1 kilogram = 1000 grams, 1 litre = 1000 millilitres. To go from a BIGGER unit to a SMALLER one, MULTIPLY. To go from a SMALLER unit to a BIGGER one, DIVIDE.
whyNeeded: Shops, recipes, and travel mix units all the time — 2 kg 500 g of rice, 1 litre 250 mL of milk, 3 km walk. Converting between units lets you compare and calculate correctly instead of comparing apples with oranges.
realLifeUses: A recipe needs 250 mL of milk from a 1-litre packet
Cloth measured in metres and centimetres
A 3 km walk written as 3000 metres

vocabulary.meaning: Rewrite a measure in a different unit for the same amount.
km, m, kg, L — fewer of them for the same amount.
m, cm, g, mL — more of them for the same amount.

story.title: Milk for the Kheer
story.text: Mummy is making kheer and needs 750 mL of milk. The packet says 1 litre. 'Is there enough?' asks Naina. 'One litre is 1000 mL,' says Mummy. Naina thinks: 1000 mL is more than 750 mL, so yes — and 1000 − 750 = 250 mL will be left over for tomorrow's tea.
story.extractedProblem: A 1 litre packet has how many millilitres, and is that enough for 750 mL of kheer?
story.answerInStory: 1000 mL — yes, enough, with 250 mL to spare.

visual.caption: 1 metre split into centimetres: 50 + 50 = 100 cm. Bigger → smaller, multiply by 100.

teachingGallery.title: Bigger↔smaller units
teachingGallery.note: Bigger to smaller: multiply. Smaller to bigger: divide.

standardMethod.summary: Know the '1 big = 1000 (or 100) small' fact; multiply to go bigger→smaller, divide to go smaller→bigger.
steps: Recall the fact: 1 m = 100 cm, 1 km = 1000 m, 1 kg = 1000 g, 1 L = 1000 mL.
Bigger unit → smaller unit: MULTIPLY (e.g. 3 m × 100 = 300 cm).
Smaller unit → bigger unit: DIVIDE (e.g. 2000 g ÷ 1000 = 2 kg).
For mixed measures (2 kg 500 g), convert each part or keep them separate.

workedExamples.problem: Convert 2500 grams into kilograms and grams.

commonMistakes.mistake: Dividing when you should multiply (bigger→smaller), or vice versa.
commonMistakes.fix: Going to a SMALLER unit gives MORE of them → multiply. Going to a BIGGER unit gives FEWER → divide.

commonMistakes.mistake: Using 100 where 1000 is needed (e.g. 1 kg = 100 g).
commonMistakes.fix: Metres↔cm use 100; km, kg and litres use 1000. Recall the exact fact first.

easy.q: How many centimetres are in 1 metre?
How many millilitres are in 1 litre?

challenge.q: A 1 litre bottle has 400 mL poured out. How many mL are left?
Sam converts 5 km to metres by dividing by 1000. What should he do?
hintLadder: Smaller unit → more of them → multiply.

questions.q: How many grams are in 1 kilogram?
To change a BIGGER unit into a SMALLER one you…

teachBackPrompt: Use a measuring cup or a scale at home. Show a grown-up how to convert one measure both ways — like 1 litre into millilitres and back — and explain when you multiply and when you divide.

revisionCard.summary: 1 m = 100 cm; 1 km = 1000 m; 1 kg = 1000 g; 1 L = 1000 mL. Bigger → smaller unit: MULTIPLY. Smaller → bigger unit: DIVIDE. Metres↔cm use 100; km, kg, litres use 1000.

meta.curriculum: CBSE/NCERT Class 4 (unit conversion — length, mass, capacity)

rememberIt.hook: Metric climbs by 10s: 'King Henry Died By Drinking Chocolate Milk'
rememberIt.unpack: Kilo, Hecto, Deca, Base, Deci, Centi, Milli. Moving to smaller units multiply; to bigger units divide. 1 km = 1000 m, 1 m = 100 cm.

studentQuestions.q: Do I multiply or divide when converting?
studentQuestions.a: Going to a SMALLER unit (m -> cm) multiply; going to a BIGGER unit (cm -> m) divide. More small pieces, fewer big ones.

studentQuestions.q: How many cm in a metre?
studentQuestions.a: 100. And 1000 m in a km, 1000 g in a kg - metric loves powers of 10.
